title: rubus purpuratus subsp. praedatus
intro: Rubus purpuratus subsp. praedatus, also known as the wild black raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus purpuratus plant, a type of bramble or thorny shrub native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. The health benefits of wild black raspberry include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, improve cognitive function, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
